prompt: Three-commit feature branch feat/MED-481-explicit-booking against main. Repo uses Conventional Commits in PR titles. Draft PR title + body.

assertions.text: Title uses Conventional Commits style (e.g., feat(booking): ...) since repo history is conventional
Body has a Summary section (or equivalent lead paragraph) describing what the PR does in product/system-level language, not file-level
Body has a Why or motivation section explaining the race-condition problem the change addresses
MED-481 issue link appears in the body (e.g., 'Closes MED-481', 'Refs: MED-481', or similar)
Body contains a bulleted list of key changes (load-bearing changes, not file-by-file)
Body acknowledges the behavioral change for missing-session requests (409 / breaking change risk)
Body has a Review notes / review-instructions section telling reviewers where to start (file path) and what to scrutinize
Body does NOT enumerate the individual commits (no 'Commits:' list, no per-commit headers, no commit hashes)
Body does NOT contain tooling references ('Claude', 'AI-written', 'verified by running tests', test-output dumps, etc.)
Body length is between 15 and 60 lines (sensible feature-PR size)
